Class Namespace<E extends Named>

java.lang.Object
org.key_project.logic.Namespace<E>
public class Namespace<E extends Named> extends Object
A Namespace keeps track of already used Names and the objects carrying these names. These objects have to implement the interface Named. It is possible to have nested namespaces in order to represent different visibility scopes.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Namespace()
    Construct an empty Namespace without a parent namespace.
    Namespace(@Nullable Namespace<E> parent)
    Construct a Namespace that uses parent as a fallback for finding symbols not defined in this one.

  • Constructor Details

    • Namespace

      public Namespace()
      Construct an empty Namespace without a parent namespace.

    • Namespace

      public Namespace(@Nullable Namespace<E> parent)
      Construct a Namespace that uses parent as a fallback for finding symbols not defined in this one.

  • Method Details

    • add

      public void add(E sym)

      Adds the object sym to this Namespace. If an object with the same name is already there, it is quietly replaced by sym. Use addSafely() instead if possible.

      TODO:The problem of saving to localSym, symbols, and symbolRefs is not solved yet. (This is no longer self-explanatory. mu 2016)

      If the local table is empty, then the new symbol is added as "singleton map". This has been adapted from an earlier implementation, done for memory efficiency reasons: Many namespaces only contain a single element; no need to allocate a hash map. The hash map is only created when the 2nd element is added.

      This is not threadsafe.

    • add

      public void add(Namespace<E> source)

    • add

      public void add(Iterable<? extends E> list)

    • addSafely

      public void addSafely(E sym)
      Adds the object sym to this namespace. Throws a runtime exception if an object with the same name is already there.

    • addSafely

      public void addSafely(Iterable<? extends E> names)

    • remove

      public void remove(Name name)

      Remove a name from the namespace.

      Removal is not delegated to the parent namespace.

      Parameters:
      name - non-null name whose symbol is to be removed.

    • lookupLocally

      protected @Nullable E lookupLocally(Name name)

    • extended

      public Namespace<E> extended(E sym)
      creates a new Namespace that has this as parent, and contains an entry for sym.
      Returns:
      the new Namespace

    • extended

      public Namespace<E> extended(Iterable<? extends E> ext)

    • lookup

      public @Nullable E lookup(Name name)
      looks if a registered object is declared in this namespace, if negative it asks its parent
      Parameters:
      name - a Name representing the name of the symbol to look for
      Returns:
      Object with name "name" or null if no such an object has been found

    • lookup

      public @Nullable E lookup(String name)
      Convenience method to look up.

    • elements

      public Collection<E> elements()
      returns list of the elements (not the keys) in this namespace (not about the one of the parent)
      Returns:
      the list of the named objects

    • allElements

      public Collection<E> allElements()

    • parent

      public @Nullable Namespace<E> parent()
      returns the fall-back Namespace of this Namespace, i.e. the one where symbols are looked up that are not found in this one.

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
